always.exam: Red flag screen (cardiac, respiratory, abdominal, cancer, fracture, infection, cord compression)
always.why: Thoracic pain has an unusually wide differential including visceral referral (cardiac, pulmonary, GI) that must be screened out before treating as musculoskeletal.

always.exam: Active thoracic ROM (flexion, extension, rotation left/right) with observation of movement quality
always.why: The thoracic spine's primary role is rotation/mobility; range and symmetry localise a mechanical versus systemic pattern.

always.exam: Rib cage / costovertebral screen (rib spring test, point tenderness, breathing expansion symmetry)
always.why: A large share of thoracic presentations are rib/costal in origin rather than pure facet/disc, and this is easily missed without a dedicated rib screen.

add.exam: Costochondral junction palpation + observation for swelling (Tietze)
add.why: Anterior/sternal chest wall pain requires differentiating costochondritis/Tietze syndrome from a cardiac or pulmonary source.

add.exam: Rib spring test + bilateral rib expansion assessment
add.why: Pain provoked by deep breathing/coughing points toward a costal/rib-cage source rather than a pure vertebral facet source.

add.exam: Point rib palpation + training-load history (rowing/throwing/repetitive coughing)
add.why: A stress-fracture risk activity in an athlete with localised point tenderness warrants specific screening before return to loaded training.

add.exam: Dermatomal band sensory mapping + lower-limb neurological screen if any radicular concern
add.why: A dermatomal band pattern raises suspicion for thoracic disc herniation/radiculopathy or intercostal neuralgia and needs differentiation from a musculoskeletal referral pattern.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes thoracic disc herniation/radiculopathy vs intercostal neuralgia

add.exam: Postural/ergonomic screen + wall angel test + foam roller thoracic extension test
add.why: An insidious postural onset is the classic pattern for both sustained-posture pain and early thoracic hypomobility, and mobility testing differentiates the two.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes postural thoracic pain syndrome vs Scheuermann's/postural kyphosis

add.exam: Percussion tenderness over spinous processes + imaging referral if positive
add.why: Minimal-trauma onset in a patient with osteoporosis risk requires screening for a vertebral compression fracture before any manual loading.

add.exam: Fracture screen (point bony tenderness, deformity, breathing mechanics) before any provocative testing
add.why: Direct trauma to the chest/rib cage requires excluding an occult rib or vertebral fracture before proceeding.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for occult fracture requiring imaging referral before proceeding

supporting.exam: Thoracic rotation and extension kinetic-chain screen (seated rotation, foam roller extension, wall angel)
supporting.why: Thoracic mobility deficits commonly drive compensatory cervical, shoulder, and lumbar overload — quantifying this guides both local and referred treatment priorities.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens thoracic mobility contribution to the broader kinetic chain

supporting.why: Quantifies baseline thoracic-related disability for goal-setting and re-assessment.

supporting.exam: Imaging (only if red flag, suspected fracture, or failed conservative management)
supporting.why: Imaging is not routine for mechanical thoracic pain; reserve for red flags, suspected fracture, or a structural diagnosis that will change management.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms structural pathology when clinically indicated

assessmentLayers.Observation: Kyphosis/scoliosis, breathing pattern, rib symmetry, scapular position
assessmentLayers.Functional screen: Seated thoracic rotation, combined extension-rotation, deep-breath excursion, thread-the-needle
assessmentLayers.STTT (Cyriax): Resisted trunk rotation; Evjenth–Gloeck breath-hold flexion to separate rib vs vertebral source
assessmentLayers.CPA (compensation pattern): Lower-trap / serratus inhibition vs pec / lat overactivity; diaphragm & core control
assessmentLayers.Kinetic chain: Thoracic extension & rotation hub, rib mobility, cervical & lumbar links, hip rotation
assessmentLayers.Fascia: Spiral line, superficial front/back line, thoracolumbar fascia
